title: config-changed hook crashes with unhandled 404 when Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs are absent
summary: On config-changed, the charm reconciles waypoint resources via KubernetesResourceManager.reconcile(), which lists existing labelled gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 objects to compute a delta. When the Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs are not installed, that list raises an uncaught httpx.HTTPStatusError 404 and the charm goes to error state (retrying every few minutes) instead of a friendly blocked status like 'Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs not available'.
why_good_first_issue: Clear, well-scoped bug with an exact error/traceback supplied by QA, and an obvious correct behaviour (catch the missing-CRD 404 and set BlockedStatus). The codebase already has the pattern to imitate: _is_waypoint_deployment_ready catches lightkube ApiError at src/charm.py:306. A beginner adds a guard around the gateway reconcile path. Slightly above trivial only because faithful repro needs a cluster without the Gateway CRDs.
affected_files: src/charm.py:490 (krm.reconcile(resources_list) in _sync_waypoint_resources) and src/charm.py:461 (prm.reconcile) reached from _sync_all_resources (src/charm.py:318/335); gateway resource def src/charm.py:69-70; no CRD/404 guard exists anywhere in src/charm.py
repro_steps: PLAN (do NOT run on the live cos VM): 1) On a cluster WITHOUT the Gateway API CRDs (no gateway.networking.k8s.io), juju deploy istio-beacon-k8s --trust (do not deploy istio-k8s which would install the CRDs). 2) Trigger config-changed (e.g. juju config istio-beacon-k8s ...). 3) Observe juju debug-log: 'httpx.HTTPStatusError: Client error 404 Not Found for url .../apis/gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1/gateways?labelSelector=...kubernetes-resource-handler-scope=istio-waypoint' and the unit in error state. Expected after fix: BlockedStatus 'Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs not available'.
repro_result: LIVE-REPRODUCED 2026-06-16 (cos VM, throwaway model, no Gateway CRDs): deploy istio-beacon-k8s 1/edge --trust -> hook failed config-changed; httpx 404 on /apis/gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1/gateways, unhandled in _sync_waypoint_resources. || needs-live-deploy (flagged for orchestrator). Static evidence confirms the bug is present and unfixed: grep for 'CRD|404|HTTPStatusError|status_code|not available' in src/charm.py returns NONE -> no missing-CRD handling exists. The unguarded path is src/charm.py:490 krm.reconcile(resources_list) (KubernetesResourceManager lists gateway.networking.k8s.io/v1 to diff), called from _sync_waypoint_resources <- _sync_all_resources (config-changed handler). The only ApiError catch (src/charm.py:306) guards the Deployment .get, not the gateway list, matching the 404-on-gateways URL in the issue. Baseline tox -e unit PASSED (exit 0).
fix_hint: Wrap the gateway-listing reconcile (krm.reconcile in _sync_waypoint_resources, and likely prm.reconcile) in try/except for lightkube ApiError / httpx.HTTPStatusError; when status_code == 404 for the Gateway API group, set ops.BlockedStatus('Kubernetes Gateway API CRDs not available') and return instead of propagating. Reuse the existing ApiError-handling style at src/charm.py:306. Add a unit test using the scenario/harness mock to simulate the 404.
notes: Real open bug filed by canonical/solutions-qa against rev 34 / channel 1/stable; manual triage but the traceback is genuine. Integration-escalation: faithful repro requires a cluster lacking the Gateway CRDs; the shared live cos model must not be used.

title: Charm can go to active without metrics-proxy being alive
summary: _sync_all_resources() calls _setup_proxy_pebble_service() and then unconditionally sets ActiveStatus(). But _setup_proxy_pebble_service() returns early (does nothing) when the metrics-proxy container cannot connect, and swallows ChangeError on replan. So the charm reports Active even when the metrics-proxy Pebble service was never set up / started.
why_good_first_issue: Small, localized status-logic fix in a single method with an unambiguous DoD ('Active only if metrics-proxy is working'). The existing code already exposes the can_connect()/replan failure points, so the fix is to surface them as WaitingStatus/BlockedStatus instead of silently continuing. Easily unit-testable with the existing scenario tests.
affected_files: src/charm.py:318-344 (_sync_all_resources sets ActiveStatus at line 344 after line 339 call); src/charm.py:172-198 (_setup_proxy_pebble_service: early return at line 176 when !can_connect, ChangeError swallowed at ~line 197)
repro_steps: PLAN (do NOT run on the live cos VM): 1) Deploy istio-beacon-k8s on a mesh model. 2) Make the metrics-proxy container unavailable / its pebble socket unreachable (e.g. before the workload container is up, or kill the proxy service) so proxy_container.can_connect() is False during config-changed. 3) Observe juju status reports active even though `juju exec --unit istio-beacon-k8s/0 -- PEBBLE_SOCKET=... /charm/bin/pebble services` shows metrics-proxy not running. Expected after fix: status is Waiting/Blocked, not Active.
repro_result: LIVE-ASSESSED 2026-06-16: not force-reproduced (needs istio installed + manually stopping the metrics-proxy pebble service; avoided repeat istio cluster-webhook churn on the shared cos node). Code-confirmed. || needs-live-deploy (flagged for orchestrator). Confirmed by code inspection that the defect is present: src/charm.py:339 calls self._setup_proxy_pebble_service(); src/charm.py:344 sets self.unit.status = ActiveStatus() with no check of proxy health in between. _setup_proxy_pebble_service (src/charm.py:172) returns at src/charm.py:176 ('if not proxy_container.can_connect(): return') without signalling failure, and the replan ChangeError is only logged (src/charm.py:196-198), so a failed/absent metrics-proxy still yields ActiveStatus. (Issue text says the method 'can return False'; the code has since drifted to return None, but the root cause -- no status check after setup -- is unchanged.) Baseline tox -e unit PASSED (exit 0).
fix_hint: Make _setup_proxy_pebble_service return a bool (or raise) indicating whether the service is up (False on !can_connect and on ChangeError), then in _sync_all_resources gate ActiveStatus on it: if proxy not ready, set ops.WaitingStatus('metrics-proxy not ready') and return before ActiveStatus. Add/extend a scenario unit test asserting non-Active status when the metrics-proxy container cannot connect.
notes: Real open Type: Bug with a precise file:line pointer in the issue. Minor drift: _setup_proxy_pebble_service now returns None rather than False, but the missing post-setup status check remains the bug. Repro tier is integration because asserting the runtime status mismatch needs a deployed unit with a broken proxy container; the static evidence above is exact.
